title: UPS Self-Test Failure
exclusions: Does not judge battery impedance projections, UPS firmware CVE posture, outlet-level PDU metrics, or predictive cooling correlations—those stay separate catalogue tracks requiring additional datasets.
evidence: Export the saved search "UPS Self-Test Failure" PDF weekly plus underlying `index=power` event samples filtered to failing `ups_name` values for auditors proving diagnostics monitoring existed.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Force an SNMP-managed UPS through an administrator-triggered runtime self-test while Splunk polls every five minutes; within two polling intervals Splunk indexes `snmp:ups:selftest` rows where `upsTestResultsSummary` reads something other than `donePass` or `test_result` differs from `passed`, proving the detection surfaces genuine diagnostic faults tied to that asset.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Repeat identical SNMP polling windows immediately after intentionally clearing alarms when the UPS LCD confirms `passed`/`donePass`; Splunk should return zero qualifying alert rows aside from benign ingestion duplicates that age out once freshness filters (`_indextime` skew checks) run.

dataSources: Route polled UPS-MIB style self-test telemetry into `index=power` with `sourcetype="snmp:ups:selftest"`, commonly emitted after configuring SNMP polling through Splunk SNMP Modular Input (**Splunkbase app ID 1537**) alongside field mappings shipped with **UPS SNMP (upsTestResultsSummary)**—confirm your deployed package keeps those sourcetype aliases consistent. Mandatory extracted dimensions for this UC include enumerated **`upsTestResultsSummary`**, outcome **`test_result`**, asset **`ups_name`**, and discriminator **`test_type`** alongside `_time`.
spl:
index=power sourcetype="snmp:ups:selftest"
| where test_result!="passed" OR upsTestResultsSummary!="donePass"
| table _time, ups_name, test_type, test_result, upsTestResultsSummary
description: This detection consumes SNMP-derived UPS diagnostics normalized into `sourcetype="snmp:ups:selftest"`, highlighting rows where `upsTestResultsSummary` is not `donePass` or `test_result` is not `passed`—patterns that accompany aborted battery or inverter qualification cycles.
value: Surfacing unsuccessful diagnostics early preserves uptime commitments by prompting proactive swap-outs or vendor corrective maintenance before an actual mains loss forces an unplanned transfer that can strand racks, chillers, or storage fabrics tied to the same critical-power segment.
implementation: Stand up SNMP polling or trap normalization so each rack UPS posts self-test summaries on a steady cadence, map them to `snmp:ups:selftest`, then schedule a saved search or alert that fires whenever failures breach your maintenance thresholds and routes tickets into your battery-replacement workflow.
visualization: Primary panel: sortable Table keyed by `ups_name`, `_time`, `test_type`, `test_result`, `upsTestResultsSummary` with conditional formatting on failures; companion Single Value showing distinct failing assets over `-24h`; optional Timeline overlay comparing successful versus failed diagnostics streams per site.

references.title: Splunk Documentation — Configure alerting on scheduled searches

knownFalsePositives:
**Facility generator qualifying drill** — Monthly loadbank exercises force UPS diagnostics into transient fault enumerations; correlate `_time` with `facility_generator_drill` tickets keyed by site and suppress matching `ups_name` rows inside approved windows.

**SNMP discovery sweep from NetOps** — Inventory scanners hammer UPS agents during audits and surface bogus failures; whitelist forwarding hosts tied to certified scanner subnets before alerting.

**Splunk Modular Input startup replay** — After restarts cached SNMP bundles replay stale summaries; drop rows where `_indextime-_time` exceeds 600 seconds unless corroborated by a fresh poll.

**Planned UPS firmware staging queue** — Maintenance consoles kick scripted self-tests ahead of upgrades that intentionally fail until calibration completes; route events tagged `vendor_calibration_pending=true` from props transforms into an informational index.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Deploy **UPS SNMP (upsTestResultsSummary)** so MIB-derived UPS diagnostics land with the sourcetype aliases referenced below; pair it with SNMP Modular Input (**Splunkbase app ID 1537**) when you need hosted polling credentials managed inside Splunk.
• Grant Splunk administrators plus facility/power SMEs the ability to edit modular-input definitions (`inputs.conf`) while restricting alert acknowledgement to the `power_operator` RBAC role group.
• Validate SNMP reachability from the Universal Forwarder tier using timed polls every 300 seconds minimum so `_time` aligns with physical test completion.

Step 1 — Configure collection
• Target `index=power` for retention parity with other facility telemetry and bind outputs to `sourcetype="snmp:ups:selftest"` exactly as your TA documents.
• Ensure extracts populate `upsTestResultsSummary`, `test_result`, `ups_name`, and `test_type`; missing keys indicate incomplete MIB transforms upstream.
• Document enterprise SNMP credentials via Splunk credential stores—not plaintext props—to satisfy rotation policies.

Step 2 — Author detection logic
Paste the catalogue SPL into Search, constrain `dispatch.earliest_time` to `-24h@h` while tuning, then promote to alert mode with `-15m` rolling windows once stable.
Compare `_time` versus `_indextime` to detect ingestion backlog skew.

Step 3 — Validate
Spot-check failing rows against the UPS local panel or embedded web summary for the same `ups_name`, epoch, and test identifier.
Cross-verify SNMP trap floods versus polled facts before acknowledging incidents.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Publish the failing-event table plus a sparkline history panel on an Observability dashboard, attach webhook routing for facility queues, and annotate known maintenance windows via lookups keyed by asset.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the physical data center for power, cooling, environment, and physical access.
